1.q: Is School's Out! free for parents?
1.a: Yes. Always free for parents. No credit card, no paywall. We may eventually charge camps for Featured placement — never families.

2.q: How do you pick which camps to list?
2.a: Every camp is manually reviewed. We check the website, confirm the ages and location, and only list operators running real programs in the neighborhoods we cover.

3.q: Does School's Out! take a cut of camp bookings?
3.a: No. We're a directory, not a broker. Parents book directly on the camp's website. Camp operators keep 100% of every booking.

4.q: Will my family data be shared?
4.a: No. Kid data stays on your device per our COPPA-aligned design. The only data we store on our server is the email you give us for closure reminders — and you can unsubscribe any time.

5.q: What if my kid's school isn't listed?
5.a: Use the city request form above. Tell us the city (and your email) and we'll email you the moment we add your school or expand coverage.

6.a: Noah (age 8) and his dad Rasheid, in Coral Gables. Noah sketched the first version on a napkin during Spring Break 2025 — when every camp was already sold out.

noah.title: Meet Noah, our 8-year-old founder
noah.p1: Noah is an 8-year-old 2nd grader in Coral Gables. Every time school was out, he watched his parents scramble. One night he looked up and asked, "Dad, why isn't there just an app?"
noah.p2: His dad Rasheid is a developer. They built the first version together. Noah picks the emojis, names the closures, writes camp blurbs, and runs the product review meetings.
noah.quote: Making an app is like making a super-fun plan, but for everyone.
noah.photoTodo: Photo coming soon — with a parent's permission.
